name: Sure.jell® Dutch Apple Pie Jam
recipeIngredient: 4 cups prepared fruit (about 1 pound Granny Smith or other tart green apples, 1/2 cup raisins and 1-1/4 cups water)
1 cup firmly packed light brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
(About 1 pound Granny Smith or other tart green apples, 1/2 cup raisins and 1-1/4 cups water)
1. Fill a large pot with water and place jars on a rack in the bottom.
2. Bring the jars to a boil for 10 minutes.
3. Place flat lids in a saucepan filled with hot water.
4. Bring the lids to a boil, then remove from heat.
5. Let stand in hot water until ready to fill; drain well before filling.
6. Peel and core apples; grind or finely chop.
7. Add raisins and water to the apples.
8. Measure 4 cups into a 6- or 8-quart saucepot.
9. Stir in lemon juice, cinnamon, and allspice.
10. In separate bowls, measure sugars (scrape excess sugar with spatula for exact measure).
11. Stir fruit pectin into the apple mixture in the saucepot.
12. Add margarine.
13. Bring the mixture to a full rolling boil on high heat, stirring constantly.
14. Quickly stir in all sugars.
15. Return to a full rolling boil and maintain it for exactly 1 minute, stirring constantly.
16. Remove from heat; skim off any foam with a metal spoon.
17. Ladle into prepared jars, filling to within 1/8 inch of the top.
18. Wipe jar rims and threads; cover with two-piece lids.
19. Screw bands tightly onto the jars.
20. Invert jars for 5 minutes, then turn them upright.
21. Allow jars to cool completely.
22. Check seals after cooling.
(OR follow the water bath method recommended by USDA.)
